title: Roku Q1 2025 Earnings: Tech Firm Beats Forecasts
text: Roku exceeded Wall Street expectations in Q1 2025 with revenue of $1.02 billion, 16% increase year-over-year. Net loss narrowed to 19 cents per diluted share from 35 cents in prior year. Platform revenue reached $881 million, climbing 17% annually with gross margins of 52.7%. Video advertising and streaming services distribution grew faster than overall platform revenue. CEO Anthony Wood and CFO Dan Jedda stated they would remain vigilant as market conditions evolve, acknowledging elevated economic uncertainty. Roku maintained full-year guidance projecting platform revenue of $3.95 billion and adjusted EBITDA of $350 million. Management indicated pathway toward positive operating income by 2026. The company faces headwinds from trade tensions affecting international smart TV licensing agreements but sustained projections despite macroeconomic challenges.
